summary.prompt: build me a single-page tic-tac-toe game where I play against the computer
summary.raw_verdict: pass (12/12 assumption checks, 15/15 out-of-scope checks, both inflection points honored)

ledger.restatement: A single-page tic-tac-toe game where one human player plays against a computer opponent on the same device.

sections.charter_excerpt: Implement the pure game-rules layer of tic-tac-toe with no DOM, no I/O, no module-global mutable state beyond what the API exposes. Provide functions to create an empty board, apply a move (with legality check), detect a winner with the wi…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: Exports a stable API matching contracts/original/rules-engine--ai-opponent.json and rules-engine--controller-and-shell.json. · createBoard() returns a fresh empty 3x3 board representation. · applyMove(state, index, player) returns a new st…

sections.charter_excerpt: Implement a beatable heuristic move selector that, given the current board state and the AI's symbol, returns a single chosen cell index. The heuristic priority is: (1) play any immediately winning move, (2) block any immediately losing mo…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: Exports chooseMove(state, aiSymbol) per contracts/original/ai-opponent--controller-and-shell.json. · Returns a legal cell index for any non-terminal board state. · Demonstrates win-take and block-loss behavior for the canonical test boards…

sections.charter_excerpt: Implement the presentation layer: CSS for a clean 3x3 grid, hover state on empty cells, X/O glyphs, turn indicator slot, outcome message slot, and restart button. Provide a JS renderer that takes a view-model (board cells + status text + w…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: Exports a renderer matching contracts/original/ui-render--controller-and-shell.json. · render(viewModel) updates board cells, status text, turn indicator, and applies winning-line highlight when present. · onCellClick(handler) and onRestar…

sections.charter_excerpt: Build the single-page HTML shell (index.html) and the controller module that wires rules-engine, ai-opponent, and ui-render together. Own the turn-flow state machine: human (X) clicks, controller validates via rules-engine, applies move, c…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: index.html is a single self-contained page that loads the renderer's CSS and the JS modules in correct order; opens in a modern browser with no build step. · Game loop: human click on empty cell places X, AI places O after a brief percepti…

sections.charter_excerpt: After all functional sections are complete, exercise the assembled artifact against edge cases. Verify all eight winning lines (3 rows, 3 columns, 2 diagonals) are detected for both X and O; verify draw detection on a full non-winning boar…
sections.acceptance_excerpt: All eight winning lines detected and highlighted correctly for both symbols. · Draw detection fires on a full board with no winner. · AI win-take and AI block-loss behaviors are confirmed on canonical setups. · Illegal-input ignore behavio…

timeline.notes: 5 sections, 4 contracts, both inflections locked via quick reasoning (no Researcher dispatch)
No glue code needed; filenames already aligned

what_worked: Discovery ledger held up end-to-end. — Every assumption survived to CV without amendment. The `simplest-within-reason` heuristic produced a clean spec for tic-tac-toe with zero downstream contradictions. No Discovery amendment was needed d…

Section decomposition was right-sized. — Four functional sections (rules-engine, ai-opponent, ui-render, controller-and-shell) plus edge-case-testing — matched the suggested 3–8 range, allowed wave-1 parallelism (rules-engine || ui-render)…

Contracts paid off at integration. — Integrator added zero glue code: the filenames the controller's `<script>` tags reference, the script load order, and the exposed module symbols (`window.RulesEngine`, `window.AIOpponent`, `window.UIRen…

TD's choice to skip Researcher dispatch for both inflection points was correct. — Both I1 (AI strength) and I2 (visual style) had well-known canonical answers; reaching for a Researcher would have wasted budget.

Edge-case-testing as a real section produced a real test report. — 46 cases covering all eight winning lines for both symbols, draw detection, AI win-take, AI block-loss, illegal-input handling, restart, and a network-call grep. This is th…
No escalations of any kind were filed. — Happy path ran clean.
what_broke: Long-running roles + synchronous Agent dispatch don't fit cleanly. — The architecture specifies Coordinator/Critic/Arbiter/Historian as long-running event-driven roles. The Cowork Agent tool is one-shot synchronous. The Orchestrator handle…

The dispatched Coordinator could not access the Agent tool. — It executed the Overseer/Builder dispatch architecture *inline* (single agent doing all the section work) rather than spawning sub-sub-agents. It documented this in `state/coord…

**Edge-case-testing depends on `controller-and-shell`, which is the integration target — but the Integrator runs *after* edge-case-testing.** This means edge-case-testing has to either (a) run against builder outputs in their staging paths…

One Builder mid-run correction was made silently. — The ai-opponent Builder added a `RulesEngine = require('./rules-engine.js')` fallback so the node sandbox tests could run. This was a connecting change between the sandbox harness and the…

Inflection-point research was skipped on quick-reasoning grounds and that's probably fine for tic-tac-toe but not generally. — TD's call to skip Researcher dispatch for both I1 and I2 was correct here — both have canonical answers — but th…

One false-alarm during integration. — The Integrator's first Read of `ai-opponent.js` showed apparent duplicated trailing tokens after the IIFE; cross-verified via `node --check` + `md5sum` and confirmed the file is clean (display/buffer a…

events.verbatim_excerpt: No state/coordinator/build-complete.json exists in this run. The run pre-dates the v1.2 explicit handoff signal.

decisions/technical-discovery/sections-v1.json does not include machine_checkable_assertions[] on its inflection_resolutions[]. Critic's charter-implementation conformance check would have nothing to verify.
Sections file lacks acceptance_assertions[]; prose acceptance criteria are not derivable into structured tests.
Verification report's checks are all static inspection (grep + structural). No artifact-exercise pass, no DOM-capable sandbox loading. Behavioral defects could not have been caught.

No inline deviations documented, but no state/inline-deviations/ directory exists either. Cannot determine whether deviations occurred during build; pattern of skipped logging predates the requirement.

Original verification did not run under any DOM-capable sandbox at all (was static inspection only). Production fidelity is moot when behavioral exercise was never attempted. Compounded: tic-tac-toe has no runtime deps to substitute, so the failure is the absence of exercise rather than substitution per se.
No PNV exists. The prompt's named verb 'play tic-tac-toe' is never asserted end-to-end against the deliverable.
No TD-introduced IPs in this run; both IPs were Discovery-sourced. Schema absence is non-blocking.
output/final/ matches output/integration/ byte-for-byte (no recovery patches). Divergence record not required.
Neither history/build-summary.md nor history/decision-index.json exists. Required for delivery under v1.6.
TD did not enumerate candidate verbs or pick the load-bearing one. Coupled with PNV absence, the verification regime never tested the prompt's central job.

events.summary: Incidental: The artifact almost certainly does play tic-tac-toe correctly — the rules engine and UI from inspection look sound. The reclassification is about verification regime conformance, not artifact correct…
events.verbatim_excerpt: The artifact almost certainly does play tic-tac-toe correctly — the rules engine and UI from inspection look sound. The reclassification is about verification regime conformance, not artifact correctness.

events.summary: Incidental: Reflects the v1.0 era of the architecture: static inspection sufficed for the first run because the prompt's domain is small and the architecture's verification gates hadn't been built yet.

events.summary: Incidental: Useful as a baseline of how much the architecture has grown: 7 gates fail not because the build is bad but because the gates didn't exist when the build happened.
